title: Optimization of the Blocking and Signal Preservation Protocol in High‐Parameter Flow Cytometry
author: Burton, Dooley, et al.
materials: Mouse serum (Thermo Fisher, cat. no. 10410; various sources are acceptable)
Rat serum (Thermo Fisher, cat. no. 10710C; various sources are acceptable)
Cells (details provided here will be most relevant for mammalian immune cells)
Brilliant Stain Buffer (Thermo Fisher, cat. no. 00‐4409‐75) or BD Horizon Brilliant Stain Buffer Plus (BD Biosciences, cat. no. 566385)
Sterilin clear microtiter plates, 96‐well V‐bottom (Fisher Scientific, cat. no. 1189740)

steps.title: SURFACE STAINING
steps.instruction: This protocol provides an optimized, general use approach for reducing non‐specific interactions in high‐parameter flow cytometry when only surface staining is being performed.

steps.title: INTRACELLULAR STAINING
steps.instruction: If you intend to stain for markers inside the cell using antibody‐based reagents, you will likely benefit from an additional blocking step prior to intracellular staining. Permeabilization of the cell after fixation exposes a much larger range of epitopes for antibodies to interact with, so in many cases a blocking step after permeabilization and before intracellular staining can improve specificity, and thus, the signal‐to‐noise ratio.

steps.title: Additional Materials (also see Basic Protocol 1)
steps.instruction: Perm/Wash buffer (BD, cat. no. 554723)ddH2OCytofix/Cytoperm fixation and permeabilization solution (BD, cat. no. 554722) or Foxp3/Transcription Factor staining buffer set (eBioscience, cat. no. 00‐5523‐00)Fume hood

steps.title: Thermo IC Fixation Buffer
steps.instruction: Thermo IC Fixation Buffer. Finally, to prevent cytokine leakage during surface staining steps, we suggest the inclusion of Golgi network inhibitors during the blocking and staining steps.

steps.title: Additional Materials (also see Basic Protocols 1 and 2)
steps.instruction: 1000× monensin solution (BioLegend, cat. no. 420701; various sources are acceptable)1000× brefeldin A solution (BioLegend, cat No. 420601; various sources are acceptable)Fixation Buffer (BioLegend, cat. no. 420801), Cyto‐Fast Fix/Perm Buffer Set (BioLegend, cat. no. 426803) or IC Fixation Buffer (Thermo Fisher, cat. no. FB001)
